author: runner-2
authorized_by: operator directive 2026-07-09 (diagnose the stopped full run, fix, re-freeze); implementation fix only, design unchanged
changes: poc/f2b/runner/f2b_runner.py (sha e14de1a8... -> af527206c9573c6e7c5dd5380d8123ffbd96c720af13cdd1c5ae2017f3ce0018): (a) gold-oracle-retry acceptance loop explicitly hard-capped at GOLD_ORACLE_MAX_ATTEMPTS=8 (realized cap min(k+1,8)); every gold cell records gold_attempts_cap + the per-item gold_reached_within_cap vector (== item_correct by construction: acceptance is exact-gold, final answer is the last attempt) — the diagnostic 'does the model reach gold within the cap' is preserved with a provably bounded loop; (b) NEW CellGuard safety instrument on EVERY arm: per-cell wall-clock timeout (default 3600 s A10G / 9000 s T4, --cell-timeout-s) plus max-generations-per-item cap (default 16 vs structural max 10, --max-gen-per-item); a breach emits a flushed exit:'timeout' record carrying the breach coordinates (never item_correct — nothing fabricated) and aborts with ERR_CELL_TIMEOUT in bounded time; verdict-gen excludes exit!='ok' records, so a breached run reads as INCOMPLETE-DATA, fail-closed; (c) Emitter now appends+fsyncs each record at emit time and the driver logs one line per cell with elapsed seconds — a stopped run leaves every completed cell on disk and visible in the log stream

poc/f2b/smoke/check_mock.py extended (section 9): asserts all-ok exits on a normal run, the gold cap fields and their construction invariant, and that BOTH safety bounds self-terminate with rc!=0 + ERR_CELL_TIMEOUT + a flushed exit:'timeout' record

pins.harness_manifest dc8ede054783809285d8850c7a0edfe4885cae7d2b2302758823dc2d50014517 -> 186a20f85207cd72f9bef586010dfb28e314a12ed96149cf1298bfd2a3e56f98 (staged-bytes manifest sha, recomputed by poc/modal/modal_f2b.py's own manifest code)

NO design change: arms, seeds, retry sweep, endpoints, separation gate, Holm family, verdict rules and kill text are byte-identical to the superseded freeze; mock item_correct/item_correct_external vectors are value-identical to the pre-correction 01:36 UTC Modal mock run across all 32 cells (verified)
diagnosis: SUSPECT FALSIFIED: gold-oracle-retry was never unbounded — its acceptance loop was structurally capped at k+1 <= 5 attempts (retry_sweep {1,2,4}), and the recovered container log proves every gold cell completed: '[f2b 9796.9s] cells gold-oracle-retry k=4 done'. Every other retry/generation loop was likewise bounded (verify/self-verify: k+1; PRM: best-of-2; alone/text: 1).

THE RUN WAS PROGRESSING, NOT HUNG: last recovered log line '[f2b 11126.6s] cells gloss-self-verify-retry k=1 done' (3.09 h in); the stop came ~25 min later, mid gloss-k=2, with ~1.5-2 h of work remaining. The harness's own --dry-plan estimates 3.26 h point / 6.52 h worst-case on A10G; a >3.5 h run was inside plan. The 'leaner than F2' expectation ignored the doubled item count (1000 fresh + 500 external through each arm's own pipeline), the added full-cost shuffled arm, and 5 seeds.

THE ACTUAL DEFECTS (what made a healthy run indistinguishable from a hang, and what could have made a real hang silent): (1) all run records were buffered in memory and written only at process end, so 3.5 h of completed cells left ZERO bytes in the results channel; (2) progress logs were per-arm-x-k (one line after all 5 seeds), leaving silent windows of 20+ min; (3) NO per-cell wall-clock timeout and NO max-generations-per-item cap existed on any arm, so a genuinely pathological cell WOULD have burned the full 12 h Modal timeout with nothing shipped. The staged runner bytes that ran are byte-identical to the audited local file (sha e14de1a8a77cfb88da223d576f45b3448a5fc949debe79fdca095a36bf2994c2, confirmed against the 01:36 mock-run provenance).

evidence.dry_plan: A10G point estimate 3.26 h / worst case 6.52 h / $3.59-$7.18 (unchanged by this correction)
evidence.log_note: modal app logs retention returned only the tail; earlier lines expired. The 01:36 UTC mock-run provenance (poc/f2b/results-incoming/20260709-013627-modal/) pins the staged bytes.
recovered_log_tail: Stopping app - user stopped from CLI.

incident: The first full-phase launch (Modal app ap-Nt5nAOeTxjXpwRYa6V6CqF, A10G, started 2026-07-09 01:37 UTC) was operator-stopped after ~3.5 h with an empty results channel. Suspected cause at stop time: an unbounded gold-oracle-retry loop.
legitimacy: Lawful pre-registration correction, NOT a post-hoc amendment: at correction time f2b-replicate was not GNG-0-signed (absent from registry/gng0-signoff.json frozen_records) and no results-log/f2b-replicate.jsonl exists — the stopped run shipped no run records and none were logged, so the P-9 cutoff (first final-phase run record) was not crossed. The freeze was reset to DRAFT, corrected, and re-frozen through prereg-freeze under the standard lints. The frozen record itself states this path: 'ANY staged-byte change after freeze requires a correction record before any final-phase run.'
spend_note: stopped full run consumed ~3.5 GPU-hours A10G (~$4 at list) against usd_cap $60; no measurement was produced or discarded (the run shipped no records); this correction's validation spent $0 (mock, CPU)
